evidence-binding: n2-assume 6483bcfc7dd7

# n2-assume — Candidate answers + falsification experiments for issue #526

## Framing & burden of proof
Question: is there a race-safe, coverage-preserving, attribution-preserving way to cut test-chain MAKESPAN via parallelism, and is the win worth the risk — or is serial the right permanent posture? Per precedence #1 (a flaky gate is the most expensive outcome), the burden is on parallelism. A candidate WINS only if n3 can PROVE all five D-523-01 constraints hold AND median makespan win > ~2× serial stddev. Else the answer is **C4 (serial)** — the default the others must overturn.

Re-verified facts the experiments rest on:
- package.json:37 — claude chain = 37 `&&`-chained cmds ending in walkthrough; codex=3, gitlab=5, gitea=5; chains are independent OS processes, no shared writable path.
- simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:12712-12728 — runSharedTmpGroup runs all 15 SHARED_TMP_NAMES members in fixed order vs ONE tmp; :12758 the shared-tmp fn is a guard-throw placeholder (member CANNOT run standalone) → carve-out is structurally enforced.
- :39-56 — runNode() env-scrub seam (strips KAOLA_*, GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null, GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1, re-adds KAOLA_ENABLE_ADAPTIVE=0); per-spawn → any new dispatcher MUST replicate on every child env.
- kaola-workflow-run-chains.js:252-287 — chains STRICTLY serial (for-loop + spawnSync shell:false); per-chain exitCode + receipt; no concurrency primitive. The C1 wiring seam.

## C1 — Cross-chain parallelism (run 4 chains concurrently)
- **Mechanism:** replace serial for-loop in run-chains.js:254 with bounded concurrent spawn (N≤4), each child own env copy; receipt aggregator re-sorts to canonical order. No walkthrough/package.json change.
- **Makespan:** floor = max(t_chain) ≈ claude ≈574s (claude dominates). Collapses the OTHER three into claude's shadow; does NOT shrink claude. Net `npm test` Σtᵢ → ≈t_claude. Low risk, smallest payoff vs the real bottleneck.
- **Falsification battery:** (#1) run 4 chains concurrently ≥10×; PASS=10/10 green + zero lock/worktree transients + no verdict flips; check no shared $TMPDIR subdir collision across editions + config.json read-only under concurrent reads. (#2) N/A — group stays serial inside each walkthrough (grep proof). (#3) `--mock-chain gitlab=false`; PASS=aggregate non-zero, receipt names exactly that chain red, others green, full buffered stderr surfaced, canonical order preserved. (#4) hyperfine -w2 -r5 serial vs concurrent, medians, win>2× stddev. (#5) change is in run-chains.js (shared script, not edition tree, no `&&` string edit) → all 4 chains green; confirm run-chains.js has no edition twin needing count-bump.
- **KILL:** falsified as WORTHWHILE if median win ≤ 2× serial stddev (saved 3-chain time indistinguishable from jitter under claude). Rarely a SAFETY kill.

## C2 — Intra-claude parallelism (37 cmds concurrent / waves, overlap the 2 heavy units)
- **Mechanism:** replace package.json:37 `&&` string with a Node worker-pool dispatcher; Wave A=~10 read-only validators (no $TMPDIR), Wave B=~24 unit tests, overlap test-adaptive-node(~122s)+walkthrough(~160s). Each child own scrubbed env + buffered output; dispatcher computes first-failure + aggregate exit.
- **Makespan:** perfect overlap floor ≈ max(122,160)+contention ≈160s vs 574s — LARGEST structural payoff (attacks the bottleneck). But floor governed by slowest single unit (walkthrough ~160s, indivisible under C2), and both heavy units spawn deep subprocess trees (test-adaptive-node ~780 spawns; walkthrough ~505 call-sites) → biggest contention risk; on 4 cores N heavy scripts saturate IO/scheduler → win can erase/invert.
- **Falsification battery:** (#1) run concurrent claude chain ≥20× (more than C1 — contention is the documented flakiness amplifier); PASS=20/20 green + zero lock/worktree transients; verify EACH child carries runNode() scrub (else dev global git config leaks into one unit not another = real cross-contamination). RACE=any non-deterministic failure = safety kill. (#2) grep proves runSharedTmpGroup still serial inside walkthrough, walkthrough is ONE child (not split). (#3) inject known failure into a validator AND into walkthrough, fail-fast; PASS=aggregate non-zero, names EXACTLY the first-failing unit, full buffered (non-interleaved) stderr, deterministic code; FAIL=interleaved / mis-attribution / "first failure" non-deterministic (under concurrency "first failure"→"first-to-complete failure" — n3 must check determinism). (#4) hyperfine -w2 -r5 serial vs concurrent at N=2 AND N=4 to find contention KNEE; medians; win>2× stddev (walkthrough ~160s, ~28% jitter ⇒ stddev ~22-45s ⇒ win must beat ~45-90s decisively). (#5) editing package.json:37 = structural change to shared `&&` runner; codex/gitlab/gitea chains shorter → propagation surface; PASS=all 4 green + no inconsistent edition chain string + route-reachability/4 contract validators pass.
- **KILL:** (a) ANY non-deterministic failure across ≥20 reps (safety kill — dominant likelihood), OR (b) best-N median win ≤ 2× serial stddev (contention payoff kill). Either → C4.

## C2′ — Constrained intra-claude: parallelize ONLY read-only validators; keep 2 heavy units serial
- **Mechanism:** run only Wave A (~10 source-read-only validators, no $TMPDIR, shallow trees) concurrently + alongside test-adaptive-node, but keep test-adaptive-node & walkthrough serial to each other. Lowest contention/race surface inside claude.
- **Makespan:** validators mostly <1s with tail (~14s/11s/9s/8s); collapsing saves tens of seconds, hides under test-adaptive-node ~122s. Floor ≈ t(adaptive-node)+t(walkthrough) ≈282s vs 574s — modest (the real 75% stays serial). Safer, weaker.
- **Falsification:** same 5-constraint battery as C2 but race test low-stakes (validators read-only). DECISIVE test = #4: PASS only if validator-tail median saving > 2× serial stddev — unlikely (tail ≈ walkthrough's ~22-45s stddev band).
- **KILL:** falsified if validator-tail saving inside serial jitter band (highly likely). Exists as a low-risk fallback to measure; if even C2′ is inside noise, strongly corroborates C4.

## C3 — Walkthrough-internal parallelism (N-worker over 231 self-contained; 15-member group = 1 serial lane) [LEADING]
- **Mechanism:** in walkthrough main() (~13000) replace serial for..of with an N-worker dispatcher: 231 sharedTmp:false scenarios (each own mkdtempSync root) dispatched concurrently; the 15 SHARED_TMP_NAMES group runs as ONE serial unit pinned to a single worker (guard-throw :12758 enforces no standalone member). Each worker child replicates runNode() scrub; per-scenario buffered output. Most complex; the ONLY option that shrinks the ~160s walkthrough.
- **Makespan:** floor ≈ max(shared-tmp group ~18s lane, longest single scenario, total_self_contained_work/N)+contention. ~75% distributed cost (testAdaptive* ~33s, testSink* ~29s, testFinalize/shared ~18s, testClosure* ~11s) across N → walkthrough ~160s → ~40-60s at N=4 IF contention doesn't dominate. Each scenario runs a full real git worktree+finalize+sink-merge → N concurrent = the worktree-registry/refs/packed-refs.lock contention class. Highest payoff-per-unit AND highest race surface.
- **Falsification battery:** (#1) DECISIVE — N=4 for ≥30 reps (most of any candidate — where real concurrent git transactions collide); PASS=30/30 green + zero lock/worktree transients; probe for (a) accidental shared $TMPDIR subdir name, (b) config.json read while another mutates env, (c) module-top process.env.KAOLA_ENABLE_ADAPTIVE=0 (:19) + testHarnessSelfCheck env mutate/restore (~12373) → in-process hazards live IF dispatcher uses worker_threads/in-process; n3 MUST confirm process-per-scenario, not in-process. (#2) run full suite under dispatcher ≥10× + assert 15 members always in SHARED_TMP_NAMES order vs same tmp, never overlapping another worker (timestamp instrument); PASS=group results byte-identical to serial + no member runs concurrent with a sibling. (#3) inject failure into ONE self-contained scenario; PASS=exit1, scenario named with full buffered output, deterministic across reps; HAZARD: walkthrough is currently FAIL-FAST (assert throws → main().catch exit1) → exact attribution; under N workers "first failure" ambiguous → n3 verifies dispatcher reproduces deterministic first-failure (fail-fast + cancel-in-flight + earliest-started failing scenario), not "whichever worker reports first." (#4) hyperfine -w2 -r5 walkthrough serial vs dispatcher at N=2,N=4; medians; win>2× stddev (~22-45s band); report knee. (#5) touches the 6-COPY walkthrough family (claude + 2 codex SKILL copies + gitlab + gitea + gitlab-codex/gitea-codex, #400) — LARGEST propagation blast radius; PASS=dispatcher propagated to all copies + all 4 chains green + edition-sync --check passes.
- **KILL:** (a) ANY non-deterministic failure across ≥30 N=4 reps (highest-probability kill per probe-knowledge §2 worktree-registry contention), OR (b) dispatcher can't reproduce deterministic first-failure (constraint #3 kill), OR (c) median win inside jitter band after contention. Any one → C4.

## C4 — Serial is the right permanent posture (null hypothesis / default)
- **Mechanism:** no code change; document in D-526-01 that serial is correct, citing falsification evidence. Operational guard stays KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS (D-512-01, 900s).
- **Makespan:** zero change; zero added complexity/race/propagation. ~574s is the price of the non-negotiable internal correctness gates.
- **Displaced ONLY IF** some C1/C2/C2′/C3 PROVES all 5 constraints AND median win>2× serial stddev. Asymmetry deliberate: a false ~40s "speedup" inside ~28% jitter (D-523 walkthrough swing 140→180s) is a flaky gate, the most expensive outcome.
- **Confirmation:** any kill criterion firing for the leading candidate; or every bottleneck-touching candidate's median win inside 2× stddev → C4 stands.

## Leading answer to attack FIRST: C3
1. Only candidate that attacks the actual bottleneck (C1 floor = unchanged claude; C2/C2′ leave the ~160s walkthrough indivisible). If a real win exists anywhere, the largest defensible one is in C3.
2. Highest race risk — N concurrent real git worktree/merge/update-ref/sink = the dominant fs/git race class; the FAIL-FAST→concurrent attribution change is its subtlest hazard. Attack highest-payoff-AND-highest-risk first: survive ≥30-rep race + deterministic-attribution + win>2× stddev ⇒ it's the answer; die (more likely per evidence) ⇒ check C1 (cheap/safe/small) clears its own bar as consolation; else C4.

**Hypothesis, not conclusion.** Probe evidence (hermetic per-scenario mkdtemp, structurally-enforced shared-tmp guard, existing runNode() scrub, sharedTmp machine-readable split) makes C3 PLAUSIBLY safe — exactly why it deserves the hardest adversarial attack, not optimistic acceptance. Strong priors against: D-523 ~28% jitter; "concurrency exposes shared-state problems" + "parallel amplifies flaky tests"; both heavy units spawn deep subprocess trees → contention can erase the win.

**Precedence note:** if n3 cannot PROVE race-safety AND a measured median win > 2× serial stddev for the leading (or any bottleneck-touching) candidate, the correct converged answer is C4 — documented "serial is right" — NOT a flaky speed-up.

## Verifier (n3) experiment sites (absolute, in worktree)
- package.json:37-40 (chain strings; C1 at npm-test aggregate, C2/C2′ at :37)
- scripts/kaola-workflow-run-chains.js:252-287 (serial loop + --chains/--mock-chain hooks; C1 seam + constraint-#3 injection harness)
- scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js: runNode() :39-56, shared-tmp carve-out :12712-12747 + guard-throw :12758, registry :12753+, main() serial ~13000 (C3 surface + constraint #2 proof site)
- docs/decisions/D-523-01.md (5 constraints :86-92, measured profile :38-58, ~28% jitter datum)
- 6-copy walkthrough family for constraint #5 (claude + plugins/kaola-workflow + gitlab/gitea + the codex SKILL copies)
